decision: Keep the Linux unit-test workflow unchanged, rerun the failed job once, and monitor the next three CI runs before changing timeout or retry settings.
problem_context: The Linux unit-test job timed out once during dependency install after a base image refresh. A single rerun passed, and no branch-local workflow or test-command change explains the failure yet.

alternatives_considered.name: Increase the workflow timeout
alternatives_considered.reason: Would weaken the current signal before confirming that the timeout threshold is actually too low.

alternatives_considered.name: Increase the retry count
alternatives_considered.reason: Would make future failures harder to interpret while the evidence is still thin.

alternatives_considered.name: Rerun once and monitor subsequent runs
alternatives_considered.reason: Preserves the current workflow signal while acknowledging that the rerun reduces urgency without proving a fix.

selected_option.name: Rerun once and monitor subsequent runs
selected_option.summary: Leave timeout and retry settings unchanged, record the rerun result, and collect the next three Linux job durations before deciding whether a workflow change is justified.

rationale: The current evidence is too weak to justify a shared workflow change. A rerun-only success suggests the failure may be transient, but it does not confirm root cause or prove the issue is fixed.

evidence_refs.summary: The initial job timed out during dependency install at 14m58s, two seconds before the current 15-minute threshold.
A single rerun passed without code or workflow changes, which lowered urgency but did not identify a cause.
The branch diff does not include Linux workflow, dependency-install, or unit-test command changes related to this job.
The Linux unit-test job already uses a 15-minute timeout, so changing the limit would affect a shared workflow path rather than a branch-local fix.

validation_run.summary: One rerun passed, but the run did not reproduce the timeout or validate a concrete fix.

remaining_risks: The timeout may recur if the image-refresh slowdown or package-registry variance continues.
A future workflow change made on this evidence alone could hide the real source of the instability.
follow_up: Collect the next three Linux unit-test job durations and failure stages before changing timeout or retry policy.
Re-open deeper CI triage if another timeout occurs with the same base image.
